title: Passive Voice
subtitle: When the doer is unknown, unimportant, or strategically backgrounded
intro: The passive voice is not an 'academic sounding' gimmick — it is a focus tool. It moves the recipient of the action into subject position when the doer is irrelevant ('The results were published in 2019'), unknown ('The car was stolen last night'), or obvious from context. In IELTS Writing Task 1 process diagrams, the passive is the default because the process matters, not the operator.

rules.heading: Formation
rules.body: Subject + form of 'be' + past participle (+ 'by' + agent). 'The policy was approved (by parliament).' The tense of 'be' carries the tense of the clause.

rules.heading: When to choose passive over active
rules.body: Doer unknown / unimportant / obvious; or when you want the recipient as topic of the sentence for cohesion.

rules.heading: Agentless passive for objectivity
rules.body: 'It is widely believed that…' / 'It has been argued that…'. A hallmark of academic hedging.

rules.heading: Passive with reporting verbs
rules.body: 'The minister is said to have resigned.' / 'The treaty is expected to be signed.' These allow you to attribute information without naming a source.

examples.sentence: First, the raw materials are delivered to the factory, where they are sorted and cleaned.
It has been argued that compulsory voting would strengthen democratic engagement.
The new regulations are expected to take effect in the next fiscal year.
Most of the growth in the sector can be attributed to increased consumer spending.
examples.note: 'Be attributed to' — a Band 7+ passive idiom.

examples.sentence: A great deal is being done to expand public transport in my city.
examples.note: Present continuous passive in spoken register.

band8_marker: Band 8 writers pair the passive with reporting verbs and hedges to produce the voice of academic authority: 'It is widely acknowledged that…', 'This phenomenon has been attributed to…', 'Concerns have been raised over…'. They also avoid stylistic overuse: they know that two or three passives per paragraph is maximum — more than that and prose becomes lifeless.

common_errors.error: 'The problem is being solved by everyone.'
common_errors.fix: If the doer is everyone, active is cleaner → 'Everyone is working on the problem.' Passive only when the doer is backgrounded.

common_errors.error: 'He was born in 1990 by his mother.'
common_errors.fix: 'Be born' is idiomatic passive without agent → 'He was born in 1990.'
'Show' takes two objects; passive → 'The data was shown on the chart.'

common_errors.error: Forgetting past participle: 'The decision was take yesterday.'

practice.title: Turn active into passive
practice.instruction: Rewrite each sentence in the passive voice, keeping the same tense.

items.prompt: Scientists have discovered a new species in the Amazon.
items.answer: A new species has been discovered (by scientists) in the Amazon.

items.prompt: The government will announce the results next week.
items.answer: The results will be announced (by the government) next week.

items.prompt: Workers are building a new bridge over the river.
items.answer: A new bridge is being built (by workers) over the river.

items.prompt: Someone stole my bicycle last night.
items.answer: My bicycle was stolen last night.
items.explanation: Past Simple passive; agent dropped because unknown.

items.prompt: They are going to publish her novel in two languages.
items.answer: Her novel is going to be published in two languages.

items.prompt: Analysts expect inflation to peak in the third quarter.
items.answer: Inflation is expected to peak in the third quarter.

items.prompt: Climate change has caused many of these shifts.
items.answer: Many of these shifts have been caused by climate change.
items.explanation: Present Perfect passive, agent retained for meaning.

items.prompt: The minister must sign the document before noon.
items.answer: The document must be signed (by the minister) before noon.

practice.title: Complete the passive
practice.instruction: Type the correct passive form of the verb in brackets.

items.prompt: The new law (introduce) ___ in January and has already reduced traffic fatalities.
Several investigations (launch) ___ since the scandal broke.
In the diagram, the bottles (clean) ___ before they (fill) ___ .
The proposal (consider) ___ by the committee at the moment.
If the policy (implement) ___ nationwide, emissions would drop.
By 2030, at least one million homes (retrofit) ___ .
It (believe) ___ that the artefact dates back two thousand years.
The contract must (review) ___ before it can be signed.

practice.title: Which voice serves the meaning?
practice.instruction: Pick the option that best fits register and focus.

items.prompt: The results of the survey ___ yesterday.
In a refinery, crude oil ___ into various fractions.
Over the past decade, ___ investment in renewable energy.
options: there has been a tripling in

items.prompt: The agreement ___ once all parties have reviewed the text.
A high proportion of the workforce ___ for more than ten years.
It is widely ___ that early intervention reduces long-term costs.
The bridge ___ after the earthquake rendered it unsafe.
Concerns ___ about the reliability of the data.
